does: This paper develops a long-term vision for advanced human society centered on the Internet of Everything and uses this blueprint to evaluate the alignment of current AI safety efforts with humanity's future needs. It constructs a technological roadmap spanning multiple stages of development, forecasts AI safety issues anticipated at each stage, and identifies gaps between present safety research and long-term requirements. The work positions itself as a perspective piece that examines whether contemporary AI safety work adequately addresses the challenges that will emerge as AI systems become increasingly integrated into interconnected global infrastructure and society.
connects: This work builds on the growing body of literature examining AI safety as a field-wide endeavor, extending earlier discussions about how incremental safety improvements relate to long-term existential or transformative risks. It engages with research on AI governance, technical AI safety, alignment, and robustness while asking broader questions about technology roadmapping and civilization-scale planning that connect to futures studies and strategic foresight. The paper responds to concerns raised by AI safety researchers that piecemeal safety solutions may not adequately prepare for scenarios involving highly integrated AI systems, building on foundational work about anticipatory governance and technology assessment.
enables: This vision paper creates a framework for reassessing research priorities in AI safety based on anticipated technological milestones rather than current capabilities alone. It enables researchers to identify neglected safety challenges that may emerge in specific future scenarios and to better coordinate efforts across the AI safety community around long-term objectives. The roadmap approach could facilitate interdisciplinary collaboration between technologists, safety researchers, policymakers, and futurists, and support more strategic resource allocation toward safety problems that matter most for the envisioned future of integrated, ubiquitous AI systems.
